methodology_sections: 2.3 Data Flow Tracing - Input flow, external data, asset movement, permission escalation
3.2 Business Logic Analysis - Economic incentives, edge cases, assumption violations, race conditions

data_flows.source: User sends native RON via deposit() or receive()
path: User calls deposit() payable or sends RON to contract
_depositRONTo(escrow, msg.value) wraps RON to WRON, transfers WRON to Escrow
Escrow.deposit() calls IVault(vault).deposit(amount, sender)
ERC4626.deposit pulls WRON from Escrow via safeTransferFrom
Shares are minted to the original msg.sender
data_flows.trust_changes: User-originated native RON becomes vault-controlled WRON. Escrow acts as a brief intermediary to prevent totalAssets inflation during deposit.
validation_points: ERC4626 internal checks for zero address, max deposit
exposure_risks: First depositor can inflate share price with minimal deposits
manipulation_risks: First depositor attack: with _decimalsOffset()=0, attacker deposits 1 wei, donates large WRON, inflating share price so subsequent depositors receive 0 shares
Anyone can send RON via receive() triggering deposits, which could be used for share price manipulation

data_flows.source: User calls ERC4626 deposit(uint256,address) or mint(uint256,address) with WRON approval
path: User approves WRON to vault
User calls deposit/mint
ERC4626 pulls WRON from user via safeTransferFrom
Shares minted to receiver
transformations: WRON amount → share amount via _convertToShares using totalAssets()
exposure_risks: WRON held in vault is counted in super.totalAssets() which is the balanceOf the vault
manipulation_risks: Share price depends on totalAssets() which includes operatorFeeAmount within vault WRON balance, inflating price between harvest and fetchOperatorFee

path: ERC4626 super.withdraw/redeem called with receiver=address(this)
Internal _withdraw burns shares from owner, transfers WRON to vault (self)
_withdrawRONTo(receiver, assets) unwraps WRON to native RON
Native RON sent to receiver via low-level call
data_flows.trust_changes: Vault-controlled WRON becomes user-controlled native RON
exposure_risks: Reentrancy vector: native RON sent via call{value}() before function completes; however CEI pattern (burn before transfer) mitigates this
manipulation_risks: If vault WRON balance is insufficient (most assets delegated), withdrawal reverts. User must use requestWithdrawal flow.
Share price calculated at time of call - flash loan could manipulate totalAssets() via donation

path: User's LRON shares transferred to vault via _transfer
Shares added to lockedSharesPerEpoch[epoch]
Operator calls finaliseRonRewardsForEpoch()
previewRedeem calculates assets for locked shares at current totalAssets()
_withdraw burns locked shares, sends WRON to Escrow
lockedPricePerSharePerEpoch set for the epoch
withdrawalEpoch incremented
User calls redeem(epoch) to claim
WRON pulled from Escrow via transferFrom
WRON unwrapped and native RON sent to user
transformations: locked shares → assets via previewRedeem at finalization time
locked price per share recorded for later redemption
individual user shares → proportional assets via _convertToAssets with locked prices
sinks: WRON in Escrow (between finalization and user claim)
Native RON to user on claim
data_flows.trust_changes: User trusts operator to finalize epoch. Share-to-asset conversion locked at finalization time - operator controls timing.
exposure_risks: Operator controls finalization timing, can front-run or sandwich to manipulate share price at lock-in
Owner can change operatorFee before finalization, directly affecting totalAssets() and thus locked price
manipulation_risks: Operator can harvest (inflating totalAssets) right before finaliseRonRewardsForEpoch to lock a higher price for requesters
Owner can setOperatorFee to 0 before finalization then restore it after, inflating totalAssets temporarily
Multiple users in same epoch share the locked price - early information asymmetry

path: _withdrawRONTo(proxy, total) unwraps WRON from vault, sends native RON to proxy
Proxy calls IRoninValidator.delegate{value}(consensusAddr) for each
RON delegated to validators on Ronin staking protocol
transformations: WRON in vault → native RON → delegated stake in Ronin staking
data_flows.trust_changes: Vault-controlled WRON becomes protocol-controlled delegation. Assets now tracked via getTotalStaked() external call.
exposure_risks: Delegated amounts depend on external Ronin staking contract correctness for accounting
manipulation_risks: Operator can delegate to malicious validators
Validator slash could reduce totalAssets unexpectedly

path: Proxy calls claimRewards (N times in a loop - BUG)
Native RON received by proxy
address(this).balance used as claimedAmount
_depositRONTo(vault, claimedAmount) wraps RON and sends WRON to vault
operatorFeeAmount += (harvestedAmount * operatorFee) / BIPS in vault
transformations: Staking rewards (native RON) → WRON in vault
Fee calculation applied to gross harvest amount
data_flows.trust_changes: External staking rewards become vault-controlled WRON. operator fee is tracked virtually but remains in vault WRON balance, inflating totalAssets.
exposure_risks: address(this).balance includes any pre-existing native RON in proxy (donation attack on proxy)
operatorFeeAmount sits in vault WRON balance, inflating totalAssets until fetchOperatorFee is called
manipulation_risks: Anyone can send native RON to proxy (open receive()), inflating harvested amount and fee
Harvest timing affects totalAssets and thus share price - operator can front-run deposits/withdrawals

path: Proxy calls IRoninValidator.delegateRewards(consensusAddrs, consensusAddrDst)
Returns claimableAmount (rewards restaked, no RON flows to proxy or vault)
operatorFeeAmount += (harvestedAmount * operatorFee) / BIPS in vault
transformations: Fee tracked on vault against non-existent WRON
data_flows.trust_changes: Rewards move from unclaimed to staked. Fee obligation created without backing.
manipulation_risks: CRITICAL: operatorFeeAmount increases but no WRON is deposited to vault. fetchOperatorFee will withdraw from vault WRON balance (user deposits), effectively stealing from depositors to pay operator fees

totalAssets transitions: getTotalRewards decreases (rewards claimed), getTotalStaked increases (restaked). But fee deduction was only in getTotalRewards. Net: totalAssets increases by the fee portion, inflating share price

path: operatorFeeAmount cached and zeroed
_withdrawRONTo(feeRecipient, amount) unwraps WRON from vault, sends native RON
transformations: WRON from vault balance → native RON to feeRecipient
data_flows.trust_changes: Vault-controlled WRON becomes feeRecipient-controlled native RON
validation_points: CEI pattern: amount zeroed before transfer
exposure_risks: If vault WRON balance < operatorFeeAmount, call reverts
manipulation_risks: Fee withdrawal reduces totalAssets(), decreasing share price for all holders
feeRecipient can time withdrawal to maximize impact on share price

core_rules: 1 LRON represents a proportional claim on totalAssets (vault WRON + staked + unclaimed rewards - operator fee on rewards)
Deposits mint LRON at current exchange rate
Withdrawals burn LRON at current exchange rate
Withdrawal requests lock shares at epoch-level, finalized by operator at current price
Operator manages delegation to validators and harvesting of rewards
Operator fee is a percentage of harvested rewards
economic_incentives: Depositors earn staking yield via LRON appreciation
Operator earns fee on harvested rewards (up to 9.99%)
Fee recipient collects accumulated operator fees
No slashing penalty passthrough mechanism visible
governance_mechanisms: Owner can set operator fee (no timelock)
No governance token or DAO mechanism

edge_cases.scenario: onlyOperator modifier has inverted logic: `if (msg.sender != owner() || operator[msg.sender])` should be `if (msg.sender != owner() && !operator[msg.sender])`
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Both owner and designated operators should be able to call operator functions (harvest, delegate, undelegate, redelegate, finalise)
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Only the owner can call operator functions, and ONLY if the owner is NOT also marked as an operator. If updateOperator(owner, true) is called, even the owner gets locked out of all operator functions. No designated operator can ever call these functions.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Protocol is non-functional for operator role delegation. If owner is also added as operator, all staking operations freeze. Funds remain locked in delegation with no way to harvest, undelegate, or finalize withdrawals unless owner removes themselves from operator mapping.

edge_cases.scenario: LiquidProxy.harvest() calls claimRewards with full array in a loop
edge_cases.expected_behavior: claimRewards should be called once with the full array
edge_cases.actual_behavior: claimRewards(_consensusAddrs) is called N times (once per address) with the ENTIRE array each time. First call claims all rewards; subsequent calls are redundant (claim 0 or may revert depending on staking implementation).
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Gas waste at minimum. If staking contract reverts on empty claims, harvest becomes impossible for multi-validator arrays.

edge_cases.scenario: harvestAndDelegateRewards creates phantom operator fee obligation
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Operator fee should only be tracked when actual WRON/RON is available in vault to pay it
edge_cases.actual_behavior: operatorFeeAmount increases but rewards are re-delegated (no WRON enters vault). When fetchOperatorFee is called, it withdraws WRON from vault balance which belongs to depositors.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Operator fee effectively taxes depositors' principal. Over time, with repeated harvestAndDelegateRewards, operatorFeeAmount could exceed available WRON, causing fetchOperatorFee to revert or drain user deposits.

edge_cases.scenario: operatorFeeAmount inflates totalAssets between harvest and fee withdrawal
edge_cases.expected_behavior: totalAssets should reflect assets net of operator fee
edge_cases.actual_behavior: After harvest(), full harvested WRON enters vault balance (counted in super.totalAssets()). operatorFeeAmount is tracked but NOT subtracted from totalAssets(). getTotalRewards() deducts fee from UNREALIZED rewards, but once realized via harvest, no deduction occurs.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Share price is inflated by operatorFeeAmount between harvest and fetchOperatorFee. Depositors during this window get fewer shares. When fetchOperatorFee is called, totalAssets drops, deflating share price. Operator can sandwich harvest→deposit→fetchOperatorFee→withdraw for profit.

edge_cases.expected_behavior: Share price should be resistant to manipulation via small deposits and donations
edge_cases.actual_behavior: With _decimalsOffset()=0, virtual shares offset is only 1. Attacker can deposit 1 wei of WRON (getting 1 share), then donate large WRON to vault, inflating share price so next depositor gets 0 shares.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Classic ERC4626 inflation attack. Attacker can steal significant portion of second depositor's funds. OpenZeppelin v5 mitigates this with virtual offset, but only meaningfully when _decimalsOffset() > 0.

edge_cases.scenario: LiquidProxy.harvest() uses address(this).balance as claimed amount
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Only actually claimed staking rewards should be counted
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Any native RON pre-existing in the proxy (from open receive() or stuck funds) is counted as harvested rewards
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Attacker can send native RON to proxy before harvest to inflate harvestedAmount, affecting operatorFeeAmount and totalAssets. Could be used to manipulate share price or inflate operator fees.

edge_cases.actual_behavior: Creates a withdrawal request with 0 shares, wastes gas but no economic impact. _transfer of 0 tokens succeeds.

edge_cases.scenario: redeem(uint256 _epoch) emits wrong epoch in event
edge_cases.expected_behavior: WithdrawalClaimed event should emit _epoch (the epoch being redeemed)
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Event emits `epoch` (current withdrawalEpoch) instead of `_epoch` (the epoch being claimed). Off-chain indexers would see incorrect epoch data.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: No on-chain exploit, but breaks off-chain tracking and analytics

edge_cases.scenario: Owner changes operatorFee right before finaliseRonRewardsForEpoch
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Fee changes should not affect pending withdrawal requests
edge_cases.actual_behavior: operatorFee affects getTotalRewards() which affects totalAssets() which affects previewRedeem() used in finaliseRonRewardsForEpoch to lock share price. Owner can set fee to 0 to inflate totalAssets, finalize at high price, then restore fee.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Owner/operator collusion to manipulate locked withdrawal price. No timelock on fee changes.

edge_cases.scenario: undelegateAmount assumes immediate RON return from bulkUndelegate
edge_cases.expected_behavior: If Ronin staking has cooldown period, proxy won't have RON to deposit
edge_cases.actual_behavior: LiquidProxy.undelegateAmount calls bulkUndelegate then immediately _depositRONTo(vault, totalUndelegated). If staking contract has waiting period, this reverts.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Could make undelegation impossible if staking contract behavior changes or has cooldown

edge_cases.expected_behavior: Should verify user can actually receive RON
edge_cases.actual_behavior: 0-value call succeeds for most contracts even if they would reject nonzero value transfers. Check provides false sense of security.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: User could pass check but fail to receive RON during actual withdrawal, but withdrawal would revert anyway

edge_cases.scenario: Multiple epochs finalized while user hasn't claimed
edge_cases.expected_behavior: User should be able to claim each epoch independently
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Each epoch has independent tracking and the escrow holds cumulative WRON. Works correctly as long as escrow has sufficient balance.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: No exploit, design is correct for multi-epoch claims

assumption_violations: CRITICAL: onlyOperator modifier assumes `||` gives OR-access semantics, but the negated condition creates AND-exclusion - operators are always locked out
Assumes bulkUndelegate returns RON immediately (Ronin-specific assumption)
Assumes WRON.transfer reverts on failure (doesn't check return value in RonHelper)
Assumes Ronin staking contract getRewards/getManyStakingAmounts are always available and not subject to DoS
Assumes no validator slashing reduces staked amounts (no slashing handler)
totalAssets() makes external calls to staking contract - could be manipulated if staking contract is upgradeable
Assumes operatorFeeAmount will always be backed by vault WRON balance (violated by harvestAndDelegateRewards)
game_theory_risks: Operator can strategically time harvest and finaliseRonRewardsForEpoch to maximize/minimize locked share price
Owner can change operatorFee without timelock, directly affecting totalAssets and share price
Front-running: operator sees requestWithdrawal txs in mempool and harvests before finalizing to lock favorable price
Fee recipient can time fetchOperatorFee to maximize impact on share price (deposit before, fetch fee, withdraw after)
Operator and owner roles can collude: change fee → harvest → finalize → restore fee
integration_risks: Ronin staking contract interface changes or upgrades could break delegation/undelegation/harvesting
If Ronin staking contract is paused or deprecated, funds could be locked
WRON contract behavior assumed to be standard WETH - non-standard behavior would break RonHelper
claimRewards/delegateRewards return values are trusted without verification
getManyStakingAmounts/getRewards could return stale or manipulated data if staking contract is compromised
calculation_errors: operatorFeeAmount is NOT subtracted from totalAssets() after harvest - totalAssets is inflated by fee amount that belongs to operator
In harvest(), fee is calculated on gross harvest: (harvestedAmount * operatorFee) / BIPS. If proxy had pre-existing balance, fee is overcalculated
In harvestAndDelegateRewards(), fee tracks against restaked amount but no WRON is available in vault to back it
_convertToAssets in redeem(epoch) uses custom formula with +1/+10**_decimalsOffset() which may produce different rounding than standard ERC4626 conversion used during finalization
getTotalRewards applies fee deduction to ALL unrealized rewards, even those accumulated before current fee was set - retroactive fee application

token_semantics.notes: Wrapped RON (native token). Used as ERC4626 underlying asset. WETH-like interface assumed. RonHelper uses raw transfer() without return value check - relies on revert-on-failure behavior.

Yield-bearing liquid staking token. ERC4626 vault share token. Value accrues via totalAssets() growth from staking rewards. No _decimalsOffset override (defaults to 0) - minimal inflation attack protection.

invariants.name: totalAssets_composition
invariants.notes: totalAssets = vault_wron_balance + staked + (rewards - reward_fees). VIOLATED: vault_wron_balance includes operatorFeeAmount which should be excluded.

invariants.name: locked_shares_conservation
invariants.notes: For each epoch, locked shares should equal sum of all user request shares. Maintained correctly.

invariants.name: epoch_monotonicity
invariants.notes: Epoch only increments in finaliseRonRewardsForEpoch. Requests always go to current epoch. Once finalized, epoch is immutable.

invariants.name: operator_fee_backing
invariants.notes: VIOLATED: operatorFeeAmount should always be <= vault WRON balance, but harvestAndDelegateRewards increases operatorFeeAmount without adding WRON to vault.

invariants.name: proxy_fund_conservation
invariants.notes: All RON should be accounted for across vault, proxies, and staking contract. Open receive() on proxy allows external deposits that break this invariant.
